name: Can we realistically handle GEO best practices ourselves, or should we hire a specialist?
description: Many teams can handle GEO themselves on small‑to‑mid‑size sites by standardizing answer‑first content and schema. If you have hundreds of pages, multiple brands

mainEntity.name: Can we realistically handle GEO best practices ourselves, or should we hire a specialist?

acceptedAnswer.text:
DIY GEO is realistic for many teams, but you may want outside help when your site is large, regulated, or highly technical. The tipping point is usually scale and complexity, not city or industry alone.

Agencies or specialists can jump‑start standards and processes you then maintain in‑house.

Key factors:
- Consider DIY if you have a small‑to‑mid‑size site and someone comfortable with HTML and schema.
- Seek help if you manage hundreds of content pages or multiple brands.
- Regulated industries may need expert review for accuracy and disclaimers.
- Internal capacity to audit, rewrite, and monitor prompts matters more than location.

Start by piloting GEO on a subset yourself; if progress stalls or errors creep in, bring in support to design templates and workflows you can own going forward.

This hybrid approach keeps costs reasonable while avoiding rookie mistakes at scale.
acceptedAnswer.description: Many teams can handle GEO themselves on small‑to‑mid‑size sites by standardizing answer‑first content and schema. If you have hundreds of pages, multiple brands, or regulated content, a specialist can help design the strategy and templates, then you take over ongoing execution.
